benchmark_context.isolation_method: taskset affinity to logical cores 8-11; no kernel isolcpus reservation
benchmark_context.hardware: 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-11600K @ 3.90GHz, logical CPUs: 12
benchmark_context.other_heavy_jobs: shared workstation under heavy contention (load ~12 on 6 physical cores) from concurrent CPU-bound jobs; absolute timings are noisy and the Go subprocess timing is dominated by go-run compile and process startup. The fastest-first ranking (rust < python < go) is robust to this load because the backends differ by orders of magnitude.

contract.timestamp_order: monotone non-decreasing; equal timestamps allowed for bursts

notes: Local non-isolated comparison benchmark only; not a production latency claim.
MIF-018 PCIe DMA ring mock has allocated Python, Rust, and Go comparison surfaces; the Python and Rust runtime paths replay 256 frames through the ring buffer.

Go is a polyglot-parity reference measured via CLI subprocess (go run); the Go scaffold has no ring buffer, so it performs 256 sequential frame encode/decode round-trips over the same frame codec and validates the summed sequence checksum (32640).
This report reflects fail-closed reserved-header, sequence-order, and timestamp-monotonic replay semantics.
